steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank falls after "their respective species" and before "the former in a repository at Washington, DC's Museum of Natural History..." The phrase after the blank is a nonrestrictive appositive — it elaborates on where each specimen is stored but lacks a main verb, so it cannot stand alone as a sentence.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — species. The: A period would create a sentence fragment: "The former in a repository at Washington, DC's Museum of Natural History and the latter in a repository at London's Natural History Museum" has no main verb and cannot stand alone. ✗
  • Choice B — species, the: A comma correctly attaches the elaborating appositive phrase to the independent clause. The appositive is not a second independent clause, so a comma (not a period or semicolon) is the right separator. ✓
  • Choice C — species; the: A semicolon requires an independent clause on both sides. What follows (“the former in a repository...”) is not an independent clause — it has no main verb. ✗
  • Choice D — species, and the: Adding "and" implies a coordinate conjunction joining two parallel structures, but the phrase that follows is not parallel to the main clause — it elaborates rather than adds a new independent idea. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
